title: rhodiola rosea subsp. borealis
intro: Rhodiola rosea subsp. borealis, also known as Arctic rhodiola or Rhodiola rosea subspecies borealis, is a subspecies of Rhodiola rosea, a perennial flowering plant that belongs to the family Crassulaceae. It is native to the Arctic tundra in Europe and Asia, and has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and adaptogenic effects. The health benefits of Rhodiola rosea subsp. borealis include reduced stress and anxiety, improved mood, and enhanced physical performance, as well as its ability to protect against oxidative stress and improve cognitive function. Rhodiola rosea subsp. borealis can be prepared in various herbal forms, including capsules, tablets, tinctures, and teas, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
